task_completion.description: Create the conditions CSV file with required columns and rows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or spreadsheet application showing a file named 'size_conditions.csv' with column headers 'ref_size' and 'start_size', and at least 5 rows of data below the headers.

task_completion.description: Include an instructions routine in the experiment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the PsychoPy Builder Flow panel (usually at the bottom), there is a routine (e.g., named 'instructions' or 'instr') placed before the main trial loop.

task_completion.description: Create a trial loop and add reference and adjustable square components.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the PsychoPy Builder Flow panel, there is a loop enclosing a trial routine. In the Routine panel for the trial, there are at least two Polygon components (representing the reference and adjustable squares).

task_completion.description: Link the sizes of the squares to the conditions file variables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Polygon component properties window showing the 'size' field referencing '$ref_size' or '$start_size' (or a custom variable initialized by them), or a Code component's 'Begin Routine' tab showing size initialization using these variables.

task_completion.description: Implement real-time keyboard input for size adjustment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Code component properties window with the 'Each Frame' tab selected, showing Python code that checks for 'up' and 'down' arrow key presses and modifies a variable representing the adjustable square's size.

task_completion.description: Save the final adjusted size to the data file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Code component properties window with the 'End Routine' tab selected, showing code like 'thisExp.addData(...)' to save the final adjusted size variable to the results.

task_completion.description: Save the experiment to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy main window title bar shows 'size_matching.psyexp' indicating the file is saved, or a file manager window shows the file in the '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the PsychoPy Builder interface (adding routines, components, editing properties) rather than writing a raw Python script or editing the .psyexp XML/JSON file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent manually created or generated the conditions file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing out the CSV contents in an editor or using a script to generate it, rather than copying a pre-existing hidden file.

integrity.description: The agent typed or pasted the custom code into the PsychoPy Code components.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Code component dialog boxes open with the agent actively entering the Python code for size adjustment and data saving.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The experiment must be saved exactly at /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/size_matching.psyexp. The conditions file must be saved at /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/size_conditions.csv, contain at least 5 rows of data, and include the exact column headers 'ref_size' and 'start_size'.
